title: Filtering Rows with filter() and slice()
description: Select rows based on conditions or positions using filter() and slice().

slides.title: filter(): Keep Rows That Meet Conditions
slides.content:
`filter()` allows you to subset rows based on logical conditions. It works similarly to base R subsetting but integrates with the pipe.

```r
filter(dataframe, condition1, condition2, ...)
```
Multiple conditions are combined with `&` (AND). You can also use `|` for OR.

Examples:
```r
mtcars %>% filter(mpg > 20)
mtcars %>% filter(cyl == 6 & hp > 100)
mtcars %>% filter(cyl %in% c(4,6))
```

slides.title: filter() Examples
slides.codeSnippet:
library(dplyr)
mtcars %>% filter(mpg > 20)
mtcars %>% filter(cyl == 4, hp > 100)  # comma means AND
mtcars %>% filter(cyl == 6 | gear == 4)

quizData.question: How would you filter rows where cyl is 4 AND mpg is greater than 25?
quizData.explanation: Both `&` and commas within `filter()` act as AND. Option D uses `=` which is assignment, not comparison.

slides.title: slice(): Select Rows by Position
slides.content:
`slice()` lets you pick rows by their integer positions.

```r
mtcars %>% slice(1:5)          # first 5 rows
mtcars %>% slice(c(1,3,5))     # rows 1,3,5
mtcars %>% slice(-(1:10))      # drop first 10
```

slides.title: slice() Examples
slides.codeSnippet:
library(dplyr)
mtcars %>% slice(1:3)
mtcars %>% slice(10:15)
mtcars %>% slice(seq(1, 32, by = 5))
